description: Canonical public source registry for research provenance and website citations.

sources.title: World Views: From Fragmentation to Integration
relevant_locators: Part I, section 2: The Seven Components of a World View

used_in.purpose: Coverage and cosmology-values boundary stress test

review.notes: Full research text and VUB publication record checked; use does not imply endorsement of universal categories.

website.citation: Aerts, D., Apostel, L., et al. (1994). World Views: From Fragmentation to Integration. VUB Press.

sources.title: World View
relevant_locators: Logico-structural model of worldview categories; category summary cross-checked through Schafer 2004

used_in.purpose: Anthropological comparison of self, other, relations, causality, space, and time

review.notes: The linked WorldCat record establishes publication metadata, not the substantive category summary; pair with schafer-2004-world-view-theory.

website.citation: Kearney, M. (1984). World View. Chandler & Sharp.

sources.title: World view theory and the conceptualisation of space in mathematics education
relevant_locators: Issue 59, pages 8-10: summary and application of Kearney's seven worldview categories

used_in.purpose: Accessible scholarly support for the Kearney category summary

review.notes: Secondary account of Kearney's framework; do not present it as the original formulation.

website.citation: Schafer, M. (2004). World view theory and the conceptualisation of space in mathematics education. Pythagoras, 59, 8-17.

sources.title: Dimensions of the Sacred: An Anatomy of the World's Beliefs
relevant_locators: Chapters 1-7: doctrinal, ritual, mythic, experiential, ethical, social, and material dimensions

used_in.purpose: Boundary check against reducing religion or worldview to doctrine alone

review.notes: Publisher description and contents checked; the ontology uses the framework as a scope warning, not a complete theory of religion.

website.citation: Smart, N. (1998). Dimensions of the Sacred: An Anatomy of the World's Beliefs. University of California Press.

used_in.purpose: Version history and public project provenance

review.notes: Project infrastructure source, not doctrinal evidence for a cosmology claim.

website.citation: Bulovic, A. (2026). Cosmological Playground [Source code]. GitHub.
